title: Alyssum minus
intro: Alyssum minus, also known as lessah or lesser alyssum, is a flowering plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its delicate white blooms and compact growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Alyssum minus include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alyssum minus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Alyssum minus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. Within this family, it belongs to the genus Alyssum, which consists of small, annual or perennial plants. This species is often referred to as a "thimble spurrey" due to its small, spurrey-like flowers. Alyssum minus is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly found growing in dry, sandy areas. It is an annual plant that produces small white or pink flowers in clusters.
